PUH-BrianMartell-Theorem 208 (Convergent E8 Lattice Block Boundaries and the Origin of Cosmic Web Mass Concentrations) to Zenodo with permanent DOI 10.5281/zenodo.19654910 and to Academia.edu. In plain English: The Three Boundaries paper already showed cosmic voids are E8 lattice tectonic blocks. Theorem 206 showed tension-limit boundaries tear and release photons. Theorem 207 added that blocks grind laterally and produce gravitational waves. Theorem 208 completes the picture with the third boundary type: convergent boundaries where blocks push together. When that happens, E8 condensate from the block interiors gets squeezed into the boundary zone. Local lattice density increases — and because mass is E8 lattice density (from earlier theorems), that accumulation is mass. Galaxy clusters at cosmic web nodes are the “mountains” formed this way — exactly like the Himalayas where India crashes into Asia.
